prompt.content:
For each class level (standard and advanced) in 9th grade biology, give me the overall weighted average final grade for each student group (504, IEP, ELL, none) and highlight any differences in the average grade comparisons between any student groups with a designation (504, IEP, ELL) against the groups without, that are consistent patterns across both levels. Please output your response (the grades and any patterns) as a message in console.

To calculate the weighted average for each student group per each class level, please first calculate each student's final grade for all students. 
To calculate each student's final grade, use a simple average of grades within each weight category (refer to the syllabus, as further noted below), and use that to calculate the weighted average of each student. Do not round figures during your calculations.
Using these per-student weighted average values, compute each group’s straight average (per class level), and express the grades as integers rounded to the nearest whole number (e.g., 80.1% = 80; 23.56% = 24).

When calculating the final grade, use the class syllabus for the weight in each category. Some of the assignments should be clear which categories they belong in. For assignments that are not clear: Case Studies = Notebook grades; Experiments and Labs = Labs; Historical Rat Study and the Rat Modeling Investigations = Bellwork; Exploring Juncos and the Letter from Lamarck = Homework; Quizzes = Classwork.

golden_responses.golden_text_response:
Average Standard Level Final Grades (Teams 1, 3, 4)
Students with no special designation: 73
Students with a 504 Plan: 78
Students with an IEP: 68
Students that are ELLs: 73

Average Advanced Level Final Grades (Teams 2,5)
Students with no special designation: 87
Students with a 504 Plan: 89
Students with an IEP: 81
Students that are ELLs: 88

In both the standard and the advanced levels, students with 504 plans have higher final grades than students with no special designation.
In both the standard and the advanced levels, students with IEPs have lower final grades than the students with no special designation.
